summary: Matched lbGx_8001E2F8 by replacing the standalone assembly file with a C implementation in src/melee/lb/lbgx.c, adding its header, switching lbgx.c to Matching in build configuration, and adjusting split/sdata2 ownership around adjacent lb files. The function sets up GX render state, loads the current camera viewing matrix, emits a five-vertex GX primitive using computed positions and repeated color/byte data, and returns true only when arg3 equals 2.
what_changed: Deleted asm/melee/lb/lbgx.s, removing the assembly implementation of lbGx_8001E2F8.
Added an 83-line C implementation of lbGx_8001E2F8 in src/melee/lb/lbgx.c.
Added src/melee/lb/lbgx.h with an include guard, platform.h include, and prototype: bool lbGx_8001E2F8(f32*, f32*, u8*, u32, f32).
Changed configure.py so melee/lb/lbgx.c is built as Matching instead of NonMatching.
Changed obj_files.mk so lbgx is linked from src/melee/lb/lbgx.c.o instead of asm/melee/lb/lbgx.s.o.

Updated config/GALE01/splits.txt to give lbgx.c an .sdata2 range at 0x804D7CB8-0x804D7CC0 and lbanim.c an .sdata2 range at 0x804D7CC0-0x804D7CC8, while shortening lb_0198.c .sdata2 to end at 0x804D7CB8.

Moved/redistributed zero-valued .sdata2 symbols around adjacent lb files: lb_804D7CB8 was removed from lb_0198.s ownership and lb_804D7CC0 was added under lbanim.s; lb_0198.s also gained a .balign 8 before its .sdata2 constants.
smart_moves: Kept the matched C close to the assembly call sequence by explicitly issuing the same GX state setup calls before emitting vertices.

Used HSD_CObjGetCurrent together with HSD_CObjGetViewingMtxPtrDirect(cobj) and GXLoadPosMtxImm rather than hard-coding the assembly's observed offset access, making the C align with existing baselib camera API usage.
Preserved matching-sensitive temporaries and ordering for floating-point calculations and byte loads, including separate temp_f*_2 variables and explicit r0/r5/r6/r7 byte variables.
Inserted an unused stack padding array, u8 _[16], which is a typical decomp matching tactic to influence stack frame layout when the high-level code otherwise matches semantics but not frame shape.
Adjusted section splits and adjacent assembly data ownership rather than treating the matched function in isolation; this kept sdata2 constants aligned with the new object boundaries.
decomp_lessons: When converting a small assembly-only file to matching C, update all three relevant build/split surfaces: source implementation, configure.py Matching status, and obj_files.mk object selection.
Matching a function that references small data constants may require moving .sdata2 ownership across neighboring files and updating config/GALE01/splits.txt, not just writing equivalent C.
GX immediate-mode code can be matched by preserving the exact setup sequence and vertex/color emission order, even if the emitted data is expressed with higher-level GXPosition* helper calls.
Camera matrix loads seen in assembly as an offset from HSD_CObjGetCurrent can often correspond to HSD_CObjGetViewingMtxPtrDirect in C.
For matching-sensitive C, explicit temporary variables can be more important than concise arithmetic expressions because they influence register allocation and instruction order.
naming_conventions: The matched function retained its address-style library name: lbGx_8001E2F8.
The new header uses the project-style lowercase include guard form _lbgx_h_.
Unknown parameters were left as generic arg0, arg1, arg2, arg3, and argf1, reflecting weak semantic evidence for more descriptive names.
Local temporaries were named after inferred compiler temporaries such as temp_f1, temp_f1_2, temp_f2_2, r0, r5, r6, and r7 to preserve matching intent rather than semantic clarity.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced the assembly file with C only after marking the object Matching and redirecting obj_files.mk from asm/melee/lb/lbgx.s.o to src/melee/lb/lbgx.c.o.
Used explicit f32 temporaries to reproduce the assembly's fmadds/fadds/fsubs ordering for position calculations.
Loaded color/byte components into separate u8 locals before repeated GXPosition2u8 calls, mirroring the assembly's lbz sequence.
Added stack padding with u8 _[16] to influence the generated stack frame.
Used literal GX enum values such as 0x98, 0xff, 0xb, and numeric state parameters, matching the assembly evidence rather than introducing unverified symbolic enum names.
Managed small-data alignment with .balign 8 and split-range edits so generated 0.0f constants landed in the expected .sdata2 ownership region.
follow_up_queries: Find other lb GX immediate-mode functions that can reuse the lbGx_8001E2F8 matching pattern of explicit GX state setup plus GXPosition* emissions.
Audit adjacent lb split ranges for remaining .sdata2 constants incorrectly owned by neighboring assembly files.
Search for HSD_CObjGetCurrent plus direct offset matrix loads that should become HSD_CObjGetViewingMtxPtrDirect in matched C.
Look for functions needing stack padding arrays or explicit byte temporaries to match GX FIFO writes.
